titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Charles Cutler Torrey Papers
titlestmt.author: Divinity Library staff

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday May 28, 2019 at 22:25

did.abstract: Correspondence, diaries, and writings document the life and work of Charles Cutler Torrey, Biblical scholar and archaeologist who taught Semitic languages at Andover Theological Seminary (1892–1900) and Yale University (1900–1932).

chronitemset.event: Received B. A. from Bowdoin College
Received B.D. from Andover Theological Seminary
Taught Semitic languages at Andover Theological Seminary
Founder and first director (1900–01) of the American School of Archaeology (later renamed the American School of Oriental Research) at Jerusalem.
Professor of Semitic Languages, Yale University
Curator, Palestinian Pottery Collection, Yale University
Chairman, Department of Semitic Languages, Yale
Associate Curator of Coins, Yale University Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, diaries, writings and notes document the life and work of Charles C. Torrey, an eminent Biblical scholar who taught at Yale University from 1900 to 1932.
Series I, Correspondence, includes letters to family members and colleagues.

The diaries of Series II date from 1880s to the 1950s. Though the entries are brief, they give an unparalleled view of the life of a Yale professor in the period 1900-1935, and the career of America's most distinguished scholar of the Semitic languages in the first half of the twentieth century.
Series III, Writings, includes published and manuscript articles and lectures.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in four series:

did.unittitle: To parents, written from Bowdoin College
To "J" [brother Joseph] and "Dear Folks" written from Germany and Switzerland
To parents, Elliot [brother], written from Germany
to "Dear People", written from Paris and Germany
To "Dear People", parents, brothers, written primarily from Middle East including Constantinople, Jerusalem
to "Dear People", Elliot, written from Middle East, primarily Jerusalem, Sidon
From George F. and Mary Moore
Letter signed by several Andover Theological Seminary students on the departure of Torrey to Yale
From unidentified correspondents, including regarding Syrian civil war, riot in Beirut
From W. K. Eddy, G. Ford, unidentified
Letters received on the occasion of Torrey's 70th birthday
From and about George M. Lamsa
Letters received on the occasion of Torrey's 90th birthday
Two notebooks related to courses and students
Full listing of articles contained in the collection
A: including "Al-Kindi's History of the Qadis of Egypt" ( American Journal of Semitic Languages and Literatures , April 1910)
B - E: including "The Beginning of the Dura Synagogue Inscription" ( Jewish Quarterly Review , 1938)
F - G: including "The Foundry of the Second Temple at Jerusalem" ( Journal of Biblical Literature , 1936), "Gold Coins of Khokand and Bukhara" ( Numismatic Notes and Monographs , 1950
H - J: including "The Influence of Second Isaiah in the Gospels and Acts" ( Journal of Biblical Literature , "Jeffery's 'Foreign Vocabulary of the Quran" ( The Moslem World , 1939)
K - M: including "Medes and Persians" ( Journal of the American Oriental Society , 1946)
N - O: including "A New Phoenician Grammar" ( Journal of the American Oriental Society , 1937), "The Older Book of Esther" ( Harvard Theological Review , 1944)
P: including "Pehlevi Seal Inscriptions From Yale Collections" ( Journal of the American Oriental Society , 1932)
Q - Se: including "The Question of the Original Language of Qoheleth" ( Jewish Quarterly Review , 1948, "Semitic Inscriptions" ( American Journal of Semitic Languages and Literatures , 1905
Si - Sy: including "The Site of Bethulia" ( Journal of the American Oriental Society , 1899)
T - Z: including "Translations made from the Original Aramaic Gospels" ( Studies in the History of Religions Presented to Crawford Howell Toy , 1912)
The Second Isaiah (NY: Charles Scribner's Sons, 1928) - annotated by James Muilenburg
"Hexatenchal Criticism" - bound volume containing notes and published material
Aramaic inscription of the Lord's Prayer
Essays in honor of Torrey: Bulletin of the American Schools of Oriental Research" , Number 132
Miscellaneous, including reviews of Torrey's work
Documents related to Alice G. Richards Estate
Correspondence of Amos Wilder with Torrey's daughter regarding her father's papers
